name: Vologda Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: purity, the snowy winters and the historic lace-making of Vologda
courage and the historic colour of the regional arms
the rivers and lakes of the region
the gold of the regional arms

description: A flag with three narrow vertical stripes of white, red and blue at the hoist beside a broad white panel; the coat of arms of Vologda Oblast is centred on the white panel.
history: The flag of Vologda Oblast was adopted on 11 June 1996. The arms reproduce the historic 18th-century arms of Vologda, with a hand emerging from clouds holding a sword and an orb.

features.symbolism: three narrow vertical stripes (white, red, blue) at the hoist beside a broad white field; the regional coat of arms is centred on the white panel.
a hand emerging from clouds holding a sword and a gold orb, on a red field, the historic arms of Vologda.
